revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:吾妻神社 (木更津市)]]
{{Infobox religious building |名称 = {{ruby|Azuma Shrine|あづまじんじゃ|qq=}} |image = File:吾妻神社・拝殿.jpg |所在地 = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|Kisarazu|en|Kisarazu|qid=Q335427|lt=Kisarazu City|1=Kisarazu}} {{ill|Azuma|en|Azuma|qid=Q346655|lt=Azuma|1=Azuma}} 2-7-55 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|23|28.3|N|139|55|11.4|E|display=inline,title|qq=}} |祭神 = {{ill|Tachibana-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|弟橘媛|lt=Tachibana-hime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=弟橘媛（オトタチバナヒメ）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11487396}} |社格 = Former Village Shrine (旧村社) |本殿 = |創建 = |例祭 = Second Saturday in July |神事 = |別名 = {{ill|Azuma-sama|ja|吾妻様|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuma-sama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |location = {{ill|Kisarazu|qid=Q335427|1=Kisarazu}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.391203|N|139.919847|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ototachibanahime|qid=Q11487396|1=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto}} }} '''Azuma Shrine''' (吾妻神社, あづまじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Azuma, {{ill|Kisarazu|en|Kisarazu|qid=Q335427|lt=Kisarazu City|1=Kisarazu}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Tachibana-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|弟橘媛（オトタチバナヒメ）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana-hime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Under the former Modern Shrine Ranking System, its Rank was that of a Village Shrine. It is a concurrent shrine of {{ill|Yatsuken Hachiman Shrine|ja|八剱八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsuken Hachiman Shrine|1=Yatsurugi Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11390599}}; you can also obtain its goshuin from that shrine. == Overview == The founding date is unknown. The enshrined deity is Tachibana-hime-no-Mikoto. According to an Edo-period sand inscription, "Kimisarazu Azuma Daimyōjin is none other than the divine shrine of Tachibana-hime-no-Mikoto," and the shrine legend records that "when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q461258}} attempted to cross from Sagami to Kazusa during his eastern expedition, a sudden storm arose at sea, and a robe of the princess, who plunged into the sea, washed ashore a few days later near this coast; thus, the shrine was built to enshrine it."<ref>[https://azumashrine.wixsite.com/website-1 由緒] - Azuma Shrine</ref><ref>[https://www.kisarazu.gr.jp/?p=we-page-entry&spot=332982&cat=13732&pageno=3&type=listtheme Azuma Shrine] - Kisarazu City Tourism Association</ref><ref>[https://maruchiba.jp/event/detail_12983.html Azuma Shrine] - Chiba Prefecture Tourism and Local Products Association</ref> == Grounds == === Kagami Pond === [[File:吾妻神社・鏡ヶ池.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Kagami Pond]] Long ago, there was an Azuma forest in this location, at the center of which lay a beautifully clear pond. It is said that Yamato Takeru and his retainers quenched their thirst with its water, using the reflective surface as a mirror to behold their weary appearances and tidy themselves. Additionally, legend tells that a mirror once used by Tachibana-hime-no-Mikoto was sunk into the pond, which thereafter came to be known as "Kagami Pond."<ref name="keidai">[https://azumashrine.wixsite.com/website-1/全商品のリスト 境内紹介] - Azuma Shrine</ref> The current Kagami Pond is a restored version of the original pond.<ref name="keidai"/> === Fujizuka === In the 3rd year of {{ill|Bunkyū|en|Bunkyū|qid=Q730266|lt=Bunkyū|1=Bunkyū}} ({{ill|1863|en|1863|qid=Q7701|lt=1863 AD|1=1863}}) on August 1, a shugendō practitioner known as Tokugyō Meishō (also known as Ichijirō) is said to have erected a Fujizuka (a mound commemorating his fulfillment of a great vow at 33°) at the foot of Mount Fuji. The mound is built from volcanic rock from Mount Fuji and bears stone monuments of the enshrined deities, {{ill|Konohananosakuyahime|ja|コノハナノサクヤヒメ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohananosakuyahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1781862}} and {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|ニニギノミコト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}, enhancing the beauty of sacred Mount Fuji and its running water below.<ref name="keidai"/> In the Heisei era, it was relocated to its present site in accordance with a prefectural road expansion project.<ref name="keidai"/> === Kinpira Shrine (Kinpira-sama) === As the sea guardian of the former Azuma Village, which developed as a fishing town, Kinpira Shrine is enshrined on the seaward side (on the right side of the approach) along with the Seven Lucky Gods.<ref name="keidai"/> === Sannō Shrine (Sannō-sama) === Sannō Shrine is enshrined on the mountainside as the deity of the mountain. Next to its small shrine building stands a stone monument of the Three Monkeys, which are believed to ward off troublesome insects. These "see no evil, speak no evil, hear no evil" monkeys are venerated as deities of {{ill|Kōshin|en|Kōshin|qid=Q3082157|lt=Kōshin|1=Kōshin}}.<ref name="keidai"/> == Transportation == * Approximately 1.5 km from the West Exit of the {{ill|JR Line|ja|JR線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Line|1=JR line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11226303}} / {{ill|Kisarazu Station|en|Kisarazu Station|qid=Q575433|lt=Kisarazu Station|1=Kisarazu Station}}. * From the Kisarazu Station West Exit, take the {{ill|Kominato Railway|ja|小湊鉄道|lt=Kominato Bus|lt_ja=小湊バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kominato Railway|1=Kominato Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17230959}} and alight at the "Azuma Shrine Mae" stop. == Footnotes == {{Reflist|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://azumashrine.wixsite.com/website-1] - Official Website * [https://www.kisarazu.gr.jp/?p=we-page-entry&spot=332982&cat=13732&pageno=3&type=listtheme] - Kisarazu City Tourism Association * [https://maruchiba.jp/event/detail_12983.html] - Chiba Prefecture Tourism and Local Products Association {{Shinto-stub|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|吾妻神社 (木更津市)|version=101840874|comment=Imported full ja history}}
